headlines.title: Trump justifies strikes on Iran amid ceasefire
headlines.snippet: US forces struck Iran in response to an alleged Iranian attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz. This action has heightened tensions between the two countries.

headlines.title: US judge holds prosecutor in contempt in Charlie Kirk murder case
headlines.snippet: A US judge found a prosecutor in contempt of court. The judge cited comments made to the media about the defendant, which violated court rules regarding public statements.

headlines.title: What is the framework agreement signed by Israel and Lebanon?
headlines.snippet: Israel and Lebanon have signed a framework agreement, brokered by the US after months of talks. However, Israel continues its occupation of Lebanese territory.

headlines.title: Hezbollah supporters block Beirut road to protest Israel-Lebanon agreement
headlines.snippet: Hezbollah supporters in Beirut protested the newly signed agreement between Israel and Lebanon. They rode motorbikes through the city, blocking roads and shouting slogans against the deal.

headlines.title: ‘Washington violated commitments’: IRGC says it targeted US forces in the region
headlines.snippet: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed responsibility for targeting US forces, warning of a broader response if aggression continues. The IRGC condemned the US strikes as a violation of commitments.

headlines.title: Iranian official condemns US attack as ‘reckless violation of ceasefire’
headlines.snippet: An Iranian official condemned the US attack as a reckless violation of a ceasefire agreement. He stated that the US president has shown no commitment to negotiation principles or the ceasefire.

headlines.title: Lebanon and Israel sign 'framework agreement' to end war
headlines.snippet: Lebanon and Israel have signed a trilateral framework agreement in Washington, ending their conflict after five rounds of US-hosted talks. The US will provide $100 million in humanitarian aid.
